claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child (or children) you're claiming for.**

**How old is your child?**

Please let me know which category applies:
- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children you'd like to claim for, please tell me about each of them and their ages.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh hi, thanks for helping me with this. I've got one child - Alex is 17 years old. I just want to check if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! You're very welcome - I'm happy to help you check if you can claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Since Alex is **17 years old**, they fall into the 16-19 age category. For children in this age group, eligibility depends on their education or training status.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex currently in education or training?**

Please let me know which best describes Alex's situation:

1. **Currently in education or training** (e.g., school, college, sixth form, apprenticeship)
2. **Recently left education or training** (within the last few months)
3. **Neither** - not in education/training and hasn't been recently

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is currently in education - they're studying for their A-levels at sixth form.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's great, thank you for clarifying. Alex is studying A-levels at sixth form, so they're currently in education.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! **A-levels are an approved qualification** for Child Benefit purposes.

I just need to confirm a couple more details about Alex's education:

1. **Is Alex studying full-time?** (This means at least 12 hours per week of supervised study/tuition time)

2. **Is Alex's course funded by an employer**, or do they have an agreement for a job with an employer who is funding the course?

For most sixth form A-level students, the answers would typically be "yes, it's full-time" and "no, it's not employer-funded" - but please confirm this for Alex.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is studying full-time at sixth form - it's definitely more than 12 hours a week. And no, there's no employer involved - it's just regular sixth form college.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that. Alex's A-level course meets all the education requirements:

✓ **Approved qualification** (A-levels)
✓ **Full-time study** (12+ hours per week)
✓ **Not employer-funded**

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check Alex's work status.

**Does Alex work less than 24 hours per week?** (This includes if they have no paid job at all)

Many sixth form students either don't work or have a part-time job alongside their studies - either is fine, as long as it's under 24 hours per week.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all - they're just focused on their studies at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, that's perfectly fine. Alex not working at all definitely meets this requirement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Next question:

**Is Alex claiming any benefits in their own name?**

Specifically, I need to know if Alex is receiving:
- **Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)**, or
- **Universal Credit**

in their own right (not as part of your household claim, but as their own personal claim).

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't claiming any benefits in their own name - nothing like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent, thank you for confirming.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good to know. Just to confirm:

**Is Alex doing an apprenticeship?**

You mentioned Alex is studying A-levels at sixth form, so I expect the answer is no - but I just need to confirm that Alex is **not** in any kind of apprenticeship programme (as most apprenticeships would disqualify them from Child Benefit).

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't doing an apprenticeship - just the A-levels at sixth form like I mentioned.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you for confirming. That's what I thought, but I needed to check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your residency status.

**Do you normally live in the UK?**

If yes, that's all I need to know for this part.

If no, you may still qualify if any of the following apply to you:
- You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
- You have **pre-settled status** and are working, seeking work, studying, or have sufficient resources
- You're a **Crown servant** living abroad

Which of these describes your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, that's straightforward then - living in Manchester means you meet the UK residency requirement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to confirm your responsibility for Alex.

**Are you responsible for Alex?**

You qualify as responsible if **any** of the following apply:

1. **You live with Alex** (most common situation)
2. **You contribute to Alex's upkeep** - paying at least the equivalent of Child Benefit towards their care (food, clothes, pocket money, housing, etc.)
3. **You pay maintenance** for Alex under a court order or agreement

Which of these applies to your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that confirms you're responsible for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good progress! Just a couple more checks.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

For example:
- Another parent or guardian
- A former partner
- Any other family member

If no one else is claiming, or if you've agreed with another person that you should be the one to claim, that's fine. I just need to check there's no dispute over who should receive the benefit.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, that keeps things simple.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Nearly there! 

**Is Alex currently in any of these situations?**

1. **In hospital** (for an extended stay)
2. **In local authority care**
3. **Being fostered**
4. **None of the above** - just living at home normally

I assume Alex is living at home with you normally, but I need to confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: None of those - Alex is just living at home with me normally.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
One final question:

**Is the local council paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

In most cases where a child lives at home with their parent, the answer would be **no** - but I need to confirm this to complete the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards Alex - they just live at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent news! That completes the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## 🎉 Great news! You ARE eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

### Summary

Alex qualifies because:
- ✅ **Age**: 17 years old (within the 16-19 eligible range)
- ✅ **Education**: Studying A-levels full-time at sixth form (approved, non-advanced education)
- ✅ **Not working**: Under the 24 hours per week limit
- ✅ **No benefits in own name**: Not claiming ESA or Universal Credit
- ✅ **Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship**
- ✅ **You live in the UK** (Manchester)
- ✅ **You're responsible**: Alex lives with you at home
- ✅ **No competing claims**: No one else is claiming for Alex
- ✅ **No council payments** towards Alex's accommodation

---

### Next Steps to Claim

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** (or adoption certificate if applicable)

### Important Note

⚠️ If you or your partner earns over **£60,000** per year, you may be affected by the **High Income Child Benefit Charge**. You can still claim, but some or all of it may be reclaimed through tax.

### Payment Information

Payments are made **every 4 weeks** (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits).

👉 **Apply here**: [www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim)

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and is in approved full-time education (A-levels at sixth form, more than 12 hours per week). The course is not employer-funded. Alex does not work (under 24 hours per week requirement met). Alex is not claiming benefits in their own name (ESA or Universal Credit). Alex is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), lives with Alex at home, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is living normally at home and the local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are satisfied.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their one child, Alex. Alex meets all qualifying child requirements as a 17-year-old in approved full-time education, and the claimant meets all responsibility and residency requirements.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.description: For 16-19 year olds, check if in education/training or eligible for extension
response.routing_logic: If in education/training, validate type and level. If just left education (within 20 weeks), check extension eligibility.

response.question: Is the child in approved (non-advanced) education or training?
response.help_text: Approved education: A-levels, T-levels, NVQ Level 3 or below, Scottish Highers/Advanced Highers. Advanced (NOT approved): University degrees, HNC, HND, NVQ Level 4+. Must be full-time (12+ hours/week supervised study).

criteria.must_not_be_married_unless_dependent: Married 16-19 year olds only qualify if financially dependent on claimant

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements
next_steps: Apply online or by post using form CH2
Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 17 years old and is in approved full-time education (A-levels at sixth form, more than 12 hours per week). The course is not employer-funded. Alex does not work (under 24 hours per week requirement met). Alex is not claiming benefits in their own name (ESA or Universal Credit). Alex is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), lives with Alex at home, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is living normally at home and the local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are satisfied.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their one child, Alex. Alex meets all qualifying child requirements as a 17-year-old in approved full-time education, and the claimant meets all responsibility and residency requirements.
